etymology.description: Depicts a newborn baby (inverted 子) with fluid lines -> representation of childbirth or newborn
etymology.interpretation: The character 㐬 represents a newborn, with the inverted 子(zǐ, 'child') depicting the upside-down position during childbirth. The three lower lines symbolize amniotic fluid, echoing its use in characters like 旒(liú, 'pendant') and 游(yóu, 'to float'), which share a fluid-related meaning.
